subject: [PATCH v2 069/110] 9p: replace PRIino with %llu/%llx format strings

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the PRIino format macro being removed and replaced with concrete format strings, specifically that the %llx format string should be used for u64 values.

reviews.author: Christian Schoenebeck
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the patch does not address the conversion of QID path from u64 to ino_t, and suggested dropping the 32-bit specific macro (QID2INO) after this change.
reviews.raw_body:
9p uses the following macro to convert the 9p network protocol's QID path from
u64 (all platforms) to ino_t. The 32-bit path of this macro should be dropped
after this change, as it would unnecessarily truncate the value to 32-bit now
[fs/9p/v9fs_vfs.h]:

#if (BITS_PER_LONG == 32)
#define QID2INO(q) ((ino_t) (((q)->path+2) ^ (((q)->path) >> 32)))
#else
#define QID2INO(q) ((ino_t) ((q)->path+2))
#endif

You are not breaking anything, if you happen to send a v3, that would be nice
to be dropped, otherwise we'll handle that on our end later on:

Reviewed-by: Christian Schoenebeck <linux_oss@crudebyte.com>

I wonder whether that exceeded Claude's context size, or if that's in line
with the prompt specified by you.

/Christian



______________________________________________________
Linux MTD discussion mailing list
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-mtd/

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch replaces the PRIino format macro with concrete format strings %llu and %llx in the 9p file system code, which is necessary because i_ino is now a u64 type.
